certificate.moduleName: Apple corecrypto Module v13.0 [Intel, User, Software, SL1]
certificate.caveat: Interim validation. When operated in approved mode
certificate.description: The Apple corecrypto User Space Module for Intel is a software cryptographic module running on a multi-chip standalone hardware device and provides services intended to protect data in transit and at rest.
testedConfigurations: macOS Ventura 13 running on iMac Pro with an Intel Xeon W-2140B (Skylake) with PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on iMac Pro with an Intel Xeon W-2140B (Skylake) without PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on iMac Pro with an Intel Xeon W-3223 (Cascade Lake) with PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on iMac Pro with an Intel Xeon W-3223 (Cascade Lake) without PAA. macOS Ventura 13 running on iMac with an Intel i5-8257U (Coffee Lake) with PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on iMac with an Intel i5-8257U (Coffee Lake) without PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on iMac with an Intel i7-10700K (Comet Lake) with PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on iMac with an Intel i7-10700K (Comet Lake) without PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on MacBook Air with an Intel i5-8210Y (Amber Lake) with PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on MacBook Air with an Intel i5-8210Y (Amber Lake) without PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on MacBook Air with an Intel i7-1060NG7 (Ice Lake) with PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on MacBook Air with an Intel i7-1060NG7 (Ice Lake) without PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on MacBook Pro with an Intel i7-8700B (Coffee Lake) with PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on MacBook Pro with an Intel i7-8700B (Coffee Lake) without PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on MacBook Pro with an Intel i9-9880H (Coffee Lake) with PAA macOS Ventura 13 running on MacBook Pro with an Intel i9-9880H (Coffee Lake) without PAA

CBC, ECB: Encryption/Decryption Note: The module does not enforce the limit of 216 encryptions with the same Triple-DES key, as required by FIPS 140-3 IG C.G.
Data that passes over port / interface
Data inputs are provided in the variables passed in the API and callable service invocations, generally through caller-supplied buffers.
Data outputs are provided in the variables passed in the API and callable service invocations, generally through caller-supplied buffers.
Control inputs which control the mode of the module are provided through dedicated parameters.

Status output is provided in return codes and through messages. Documentation for each API lists possible return codes. A complete list of all return codes returned by the C language APIs within the module is provided in the header files and the API documentation. Messages are also documented in the API documentation.

The seed is provided by post-processed entropy data from two entropy sources. The entropy sources are located within the physical perimeter of the module but outside the cryptographic boundary of the module.
